title: fix(model-monitor): register embeddings, split status into its own column
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
The model monitor was showing embedding models (`openai-3-large`, `gemini-2`) as "Unknown / Unregistered model" because the hook only knew about text/image/audio catalogs. Tinybird emits `generate.embedding` traffic for those, so they fell through to the unregistered branch.

- Import `EMBEDDING_SERVICES` and include it in `ALL_REGISTERED_MODELS` so `generate.embedding` rows merge with registry metadata
- Fetch `/embeddings/models` alongside the other catalogs and track its endpoint status (drives the fallback banner)
- Add an "Embedding" health-summary card using the existing `tan` tones — no new palette tokens
- Pull `StatusBadge` (OFF/DEGRADED) and `CatalogStatusBadge` (UNKNOWN/ANOMALY/UNVERIFIED/REGISTRY/HIDDEN) out of the Model cell into a sortable **Status** column, ranked by severity (off > degraded > unknown > anomaly > unverified > registry > hidden) so problems bubble to the top

Hidden models left untouched.

gist.headline: Embeddings join the watch
gist.blurb: The model monitor now tracks embedding models properly and features a brand new sortable status column to bubble up issues faster.
gist.summary: The model monitor now registers embedding models like openai-3-large and gemini-2 to prevent them from showing as unknown, and extracts model status into a dedicated, severity-ranked column.
gist.impact: Developers checking the monitor will now see accurate health cards for embedding models and can sort by status to quickly spot degraded or offline services.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee and the Monitor Robot looking at a dashboard/screen. The screen shows a list with a new 'Status' column and a tan-colored 'Embeddings' card. The robot is happy to see the new data.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot and a chunky monitor-head robot looking at a glowing retro dashboard screen. The screen displays a neat list with a highlighted 'Status' column and a tan-colored 'Embeddings' card. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and warm peach gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
